title: TritonAI Roadmap
description: A public, status-based view of the TritonAI ecosystem from the 2023 chatbot launch through the 2026 governed builder path. Recent milestones include initiative-level detail drawn from the public TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and 2026 quarterly roadmap.
source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and AI Roadmap 2026 Q1-Q3 (presentation-test/ucsd-presentation, public-safe excerpts) and public product pages

items.title: Beta & Training
items.summary: Secured Chancellor's Office funding, invited 400+ staff to the campus beta, and launched AI Essentials I in the UC Learning Center.

items.title: Early Launch
items.summary: Began the phased rollout and launched the UC San Diego Assistant and Job Description Helper.

items.title: Campus-Wide
items.summary: Opened campus-wide availability for ~37K employees, upgraded to Llama 3 with campus SSO, and refreshed the UI with file upload.
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and TritonGPT release notes

items.title: Adoption
items.summary: Grew Fund Manager Coach adoption, expanded departmental use cases, and began infrastructure planning for the next platform phase.

items.title: Platform Prep
items.summary: Upgraded the platform architecture for scale and prepared the model gateway for both commercial and local LLMs.

items.title: Universal Access
items.summary: Onboarded 45K students, made GPT-4o and Llama 4 Scout the default models, and launched the 128K context window with My Documents.
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and public release notes

items.title: Widget & Infra
items.summary: Deployed the Blink AI widget without a login requirement and integrated LiteLLM to enable model choice.

items.title: Advanced Tools
items.summary: Launched AI-powered search and the Blink AI overview, expanded model choice for higher-value use cases, and began the Deep Research and chat UI refresh.
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and public release notes

items.title: Ecosystem Expansion
items.summary: Launched transcript OCR and student record matching, expanded instructional AI to 8 courses with scheduling assistant pilots, and opened the TritonAI site with API access and early N8N workflows.
details: Transcript verification moved from an OCR and matching proof of concept toward an OnBase handoff for enrollment operations.
Instructional AI added one-click assistant creation while scheduling and spring-course pilots continued.
Contract review expanded as a supervised Procurement workflow with configurable, policy-backed redlining.
The TritonAI site and Developer APIs opened governed model access for campus builders.
Research proposal risk and research alignment proofs of concept tested focused research-support workflows.
items.owner: TritonAI solutions team and sponsoring units
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and AI Roadmap 2026 — Quarter 1

items.title: Process Automation
items.summary: Refined contract review with adjustable redlining, piloted an Academic Advising Agent proof of concept, and launched an AI IT observability pilot.
details: Developer API access and N8N Agent Builder broadened the path from model access to supervised workflow orchestration.
TritonGPT extended into the campus mobile experience and began surfacing live campus signals with stronger response traceability.
A Canvas-integrated instructional assistant established a reusable delivery pattern for course support.
Transcript matching advanced toward admissions launch readiness while contract review added adjustable rule sets.
BioBib formatting, risk-based proposal review, and AI IT observability expanded the bounded prototype portfolio.
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and AI Roadmap 2026 — Quarter 2

items.title: Governed Builder Path
items.summary: Connecting Developer APIs, Agent Builder, and the skills library to reusable capabilities; governing data access and tool calls with MCP connectors and observability; and turning successful prototypes into supported campus tools via Campus App Hosting.
details: A bounded Citizen Developer pilot is testing a gateway-connected harness, tiered model access, and early project accountability patterns.
Campus App Hosting is beginning with limited-scope proof-of-concept apps and a shared-responsibility path to support.
The skills pipeline is organizing reusable UC San Diego skills, MCP integrations, and data-source patterns.
Academic advising and shared knowledge-management work remain in scoped prototype and build phases.
Governance and observability work is documenting data, security, reliability, and operational requirements before broader access.
items.source: TritonAI Ecosystem Timeline and AI Roadmap 2026 — Quarter 3

items.title: Governed agency at campus scale
items.summary: Evaluating repeatable, supervised agent workflows that can use approved tools and connectors while retaining accountable human review.
details: Agents would retrieve approved data and call approved tools only within bounded workflows.
Human review would remain at consequential decisions and actions.
Observability would capture the reliability, usage, and evidence needed to operate services responsibly.
Successful pilots would graduate through a named service owner, governance review, support model, and approved hosting path.
items.owner: TritonAI program and campus governance partners
items.source: TritonAI strategy presentation and From Chat to Agents
